issues_found: Source is a brief summary/abstract rather than full survey results; lacks detailed quantitative data
llm_review.verification_confidence is set to 0 but should reflect actual verification assessment
human_review_reason: Record is accurate and faithful to the source, but borderline case—source is thin (abstract only) and verification confidence is 7. Auto-routing to review queue is appropriate without requiring human escalation.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This survey provides forward-looking indicators on Canadian economic activity, employment intentions, and price pressures. Subdued sentiment combined with plans to reduce staffing and limit investment suggests weak domestic demand ahead. Stable but elevated inflation expectations (2.5-3%) give the Bank of Canada room to monitor for demand weakness. Bond and CAD traders will watch for confirmation that the soft patch is temporary versus structural.
corrected_fields.macro_context: Canadian business sentiment remains weak with muted hiring and investment intentions. The slight expected improvement in domestic sales after a weak year indicates the economy is not deteriorating further but lacks momentum for strong growth. Inflation expectations at 2.5-3% remain above the Bank of Canada's 2% target, suggesting the BoC will remain cautious about easing further. Reduced tariff-related cost pressures and limited pricing power suggest inflation will remain contained, supporting a patient monetary policy approach.
